summary: The generated tests are entirely speculative — they assume a specific module (extensions/plan-feature.ts), a specific export (__testables.formatPlanningBanner), and a specific function signature that are nowhere mentioned in the plan. The plan itself is extremely vague (it only says to 'review and update' an existing test file) and provides no concrete module, function, or API. The tests fabricate an API contract from thin air rather than testing real behavior grounded in the repository.

findings.title: Tests target a fabricated API (formatPlanningBanner) not mentioned in plan or repo
findings.details: The plan never mentions extensions/plan-feature.ts, __testables, or formatPlanningBanner. The generator metadata acknowledges this ('The plan does not specify which source module contains the banner rendering logic — we infer extensions/plan-feature.ts from Example 2'). Every single test depends on importing this non-existent function. There is no evidence in the repository context that plan-feature.ts exists or that __testables.formatPlanningBanner will be the implementation approach. This makes the entire suite speculative and non-executable against the actual codebase.
findings.fix: Report the plan as too ambiguous to generate meaningful tests. Either flag a blocking ambiguity finding requiring plan clarification (specifying the target module, function signature, and integration point), or if generating tests, target the actual file mentioned in the plan scope (__tests__/extensions/currently-it-is-not-possible-for-me-to-work-easily-on-the-sa.plan.test.ts) and test observable CLI output behavior.
findings.requirement: Edge case: No obvious existing module is found for the request.
evidence: Plan scope says only: 'Review and update __tests__/extensions/currently-it-is-not-possible-for-me-to-work-easily-on-the-sa.plan.test.ts'
Generator note: 'The plan does not specify which source module contains the banner rendering logic'
Plan edge case explicitly states: 'No obvious existing module is found for the request'

findings.title: Plan is too vague to derive testable requirements — should be flagged as blocking
findings.details: The plan provides no concrete acceptance criteria about code behavior. Its acceptance criteria are meta-level ('The feature behavior is documented in repo-grounded terms', 'The plan cites affected modules'). The scope only mentions reviewing an existing test file. The plan's own edge cases acknowledge 'Critical behavior remains ambiguous and needs clarification before implementation.' The generator acknowledged 8 ambiguous requirements but proceeded to generate tests anyway based on assumptions. This should result in a verdict that the plan needs clarification, not in fabricated tests.
findings.fix: Return a test suite that clearly documents the ambiguities as failing/skipped tests with descriptive messages, or decline to generate behavioral tests until the plan specifies: (1) which module contains the banner rendering, (2) what function/API to test, (3) how the banner is integrated into the CLI output.
findings.requirement: Edge case: Critical behavior remains ambiguous and needs clarification before implementation.
evidence: Plan edge case: 'Critical behavior remains ambiguous and needs clarification before implementation'
Plan acceptance criteria are all meta-level, not behavioral

findings.title: Plan scope item 'Review and update existing test file' is not addressed
findings.details: The plan's only concrete scope item is 'Review and update __tests__/extensions/currently-it-is-not-possible-for-me-to-work-easily-on-the-sa.plan.test.ts'. The generated tests create an entirely new test file instead of reviewing/updating the existing one. The generator notes claim this is addressed by 'aligning conventions' but the output is a brand new file with fabricated imports, not a review or update of the existing file.
findings.fix: If generating tests, first inspect the contents of the existing test file mentioned in the plan scope and base the test structure on what is actually there, rather than fabricating new modules.
evidence: Generated output path is a completely different file

findings.title: Test uses node:test but generator metadata says vitest
findings.details: Generator metadata declares framework as 'vitest' but the generated tests use 'import test from "node:test"' and 'import assert from "node:assert/strict"'. This mismatch could cause execution issues depending on the test runner configuration. The repository context shows no test configuration files and no scripts, so it's unclear which runner would actually be used.
findings.fix: Pick one test framework consistently. If using vitest (as declared), use vitest imports (describe, it, expect). If using node:test, update the metadata accordingly.
evidence: Generated code: import test from "node:test"; import assert from "node:assert/strict";

findings.title: Missing major behavioral requirement
findings.details: No generated coverage entry clearly maps to: Review and update __tests__/extensions/currently-it-is-not-possible-for-me-to-work-easily-on-the-sa.plan.test.ts
findings.fix: Add an explicit behavioral test for this requirement or explain why it cannot be tested realistically.

strengths: Dynamic import with try/catch is a reasonable defensive pattern for TDD where the module doesn't exist yet
ANSI detection helpers are well-crafted and cover multiple escape code variants
The generator was thorough in documenting ambiguities in the metadata
Edge cases (empty title, inactive session) are reasonable if the API existed
The regression guard test for existing state management functions gracefully skips if they don't exist
